authority: release-decision contract only; not candidate admission, manifest mutation, release, installation, cross-repository, commit, or push authority

problem.drift: requiring at least one new approved Skill creates a count-growth quota that conflicts with residual-gap-only authoring, selective admission, user value, and the current zero-supported-gap result

replacement.principle: the approved release evolves only when reviewed evidence produces positive net value; unchanged is a valid result

allowedOutcomes.condition: no candidate or composition proves material net benefit over current native, runtime, curated, recipe, non-Skill, fallback, or human-authority paths
a reviewed candidate fills a supported demand or residual gap and passes provenance, license, security, portability, overlap, validation, and admission gates
a reviewed external or repository-authored capability is materially safer, better, simpler, or cheaper than current approved payload
existing native, runtime, curated, or non-Skill capabilities cover the demand better as a recipe or routing decision without new payload admission
approved payload becomes unsafe, stale, redundant, inferior, incompatible, unmaintained, or no longer worth its lifecycle cost

invariants: raw Skill count, repository count, stars, suite breadth, and installability are not success metrics
zero supported residual gaps never creates an obligation to admit or author a Skill
an unchanged release after a governed comparison is a valid evidence-backed outcome
external work may replace or supersede repository-authored Skills when evidence is stronger
composition, native reasoning, runtime capability, non-Skill mechanisms, human authority, and no additional structure remain valid outcomes
every add, replace, supersede, deprecate, or retire decision preserves provenance, migration, rollback, and separate admission authority

currentDecision.nextGate: re-evaluate only after a new supported demand, material candidate advantage, approved-payload drift, lifecycle risk, consumer feedback, or runtime change can alter the release decision

recheckTriggers: a new source-supported demand or repeated current-path failure
a candidate demonstrates material quality, safety, maintenance, portability, cost, or coverage advantage
an approved source, license, dependency, executable surface, maintainer state, or upstream revision changes materially
consumer feedback or cross-host evidence changes benefit, collision, context, or lifecycle cost
a release add, replacement, supersession, deprecation, retirement, or repository-authored gap fill is proposed
nonActions: no release-manifest or approved payload mutation
no live Agent, CC Switch, user-configuration, or runtime mutation
no cross-repository write, commit, or push
